info.description: The Monster Hunter World API provides detailed information about the game, including items, monsters, armor, skills, events, and more. It allows users to access various resources related to the game in a structured format, facilitating data retrieval and integration into applications.
info.title: Monster Hunter World API
info.tagline: Query Monster Hunter World game data — monsters, weapons, armor, skills, items, and events
info.about_md:
The [Monster Hunter World API](https://docs.mhw-db.com/) is a community-maintained REST service that exposes the equipment, creatures, skills, and game mechanics from Capcom's *Monster Hunter: World*. The project has entered maintenance mode and will not receive further feature work, but the endpoints remain available at `https://mhw-db.com`.

What you get from the API:

- Monsters with weaknesses, ailments, and drop tables
- All 14 weapon types with detailed stats and motion values
- Armor pieces, full armor sets, and set bonuses
- Charms with upgrade trees, decorations (jewels), and skill modifiers
- Items (consumables, crafting materials), locations with camp sites, and limited-time events

No authentication is required and no rate limits are documented. CORS is disabled, so calls generally need to go through a server-side proxy rather than directly from a browser. Responses can be localized to English (default), French, German, Simplified Chinese, or Traditional Chinese via optional language tag prefixes in the URL path. A successor project for *Monster Hunter Wilds* is available at [docs.wilds.mhdb.io](https://docs.wilds.mhdb.io).

entity_desc.ailment: Status effects (poison, paralysis, etc.) along with recovery items and methods.
entity_desc.armor: Individual armor pieces with stats, skills, and crafting requirements.
entity_desc.armor_set: Full armor sets that group pieces and expose set bonuses.
entity_desc.charm: Equippable charms with their upgrade trees and granted skills.
entity_desc.decoration: Jewels that slot into armor or weapons to grant additional skill points.
entity_desc.event: Limited-time in-game quests and event metadata.
entity_desc.item: Consumables and crafting materials used throughout the game.
entity_desc.location: Maps and their camp sites where hunts take place.
entity_desc.monster: Enemy creatures with elemental weaknesses, ailment susceptibilities, and drop tables.
entity_desc.motion_value: Per-attack motion-value data describing how weapon moves scale damage.
entity_desc.skill: Player ability modifiers granted by armor, charms, and decorations.
entity_desc.weapon: Entries for all 14 weapon types with sharpness, element, and stat details.

test.name: test
test.title: In-memory mock transport for testing without a live server
